subject: Project Management
title: Kanban in Manufacturing Today
description: A quick, practical guide to Kanban in Manufacturing Today.

hook.text: Toyota still uses physical kanban cards. After 70 years, the original system still works.

buildup.text: Modern factories blend physical cards with electronic signals — e-kanban — for speed.

discovery.text: Automotive, electronics, and food production all run on kanban-based pull systems.

twist.text: COVID exposed kanban's weakness: JIT with no buffer fails when global supply chains collapse.

climax.text: Post-COVID, factories are adding strategic buffers — just-in-case alongside just-in-time.

punchline.text: Even the best system needs a Plan B.

quiz.question: How has COVID affected kanban-based manufacturing?
options: Factories are adding strategic buffers alongside just-in-time
